title: Interesting Times: Why Are We Still Driving?
description: Confronting the weirdness of a Waymo future.
author: Ross Douthat and Sophia Alvarez Boyd

title: I Tried to Sell My House With A.I.
description: Over five frantic days, I gambled my family’s life savings on a hunch that A.I. could outperform a real estate agent.
author: Stuart A. Thompson

title: Anthropic Tops OpenAI to Become the World’s Most Valuable A.I. Start-Up
description: Anthropic raised $65 billion in new fund-raising that put its value at $900 billion, ahead of OpenAI’s last valuation of $730 billion, as the companies duel for A.I. dominance.
author: Mike Isaac and Cade Metz

title: A.I. Is Making Scams Hard to Spot. Here’s How to Protect Yourself.
description: A criminal could be masquerading as a celebrity, web store or family member asking for your money. Detecting scams requires a new approach.
author: Brian X. Chen

title: In Argentina, U.S. Tech Billionaire Peter Thiel Finds An Escape
description: The billionaire’s new roots in Argentina are said to be partly motivated by concerns about the future of the United States and shared beliefs with Argentina’s right-wing leader.
author: Emma Bubola and Ryan Mac

title: Spencer Pratt Wants to Be Mayor. His Fans Want Him to Be Batman.
description: What an A.I.-generated video of the former reality TV star teaches us about the future of political ads.
author: Willy Staley

title: Teachers Union Urges Schools to Curb A.I. Chatbots and Screen Time
description: The American Federation of Teachers recommended “no screens” at all for those in second grade or younger, and no A.I. chatbots for students in elementary school.
author: Natasha Singer

title: Go Ask Alice Why Tech Start-Ups Are Spending Big on Hype Videos
description: A Mad Hatter and a giant rabbit sit around a table discussing an A.I. start-up. This is normal behavior around the Bay Area these days.
author: Natallie Rocha and Mike Kai Chen

title: At the A.I. Epicenter, Technologists Dismiss Pope Leo’s Warnings About the New Technology
description: Pope Leo XIV’s spiritual message on artificial intelligence arrived as Silicon Valley’s A.I. enthusiasts pursue their own spirituality through technology.
author: Cade Metz

title: SpaceX IPO Filing Reveals Favorable Terms for Elon Musk
description: The ways it set up its board and Mr. Musk’s pay appear to benefit him at the expense of other shareholders, corporate governance experts said.
author: Ryan Mac

title: ‘The Maids,’ With Yerin Ha, Asks: Has Life Become One Big Performance?
description: In his rethinking of Jean Genet’s classic work about class and power, Kip Williams ponders “a world that gives you every opportunity not to be yourself.”
author: Roslyn Sulcas

title: Massachusetts Uber and Lyft Drivers Form First Union for Ride-Share Workers
description: The App Drivers Union said it was the first organization in the country to be formally certified to represent drivers for apps such as Uber and Lyft.
author: Amanda Holpuch

title: Waymo Suspends Service in Six Cities After Cars Drove Into Flooded Roads
description: The temporary shutdowns came after videos emerged showing two Waymo cars stopped on swamped streets in Atlanta on Wednesday.
author: Michael Levenson

title: Google Appeals Landmark Ruling Declaring It a Monopolist in Search
description: Google asked a federal appeals court on Friday to reverse a judge’s ruling that it had violated antitrust laws, as well as a requirement it share some data with rivals.
author: David McCabe

title: Sundar Pichai Understands Why People Are Anxious About A.I.
description: After a busy Google I/O, the company’s chief executive sits down with the hosts of “Hard Fork” to discuss the future of Google Search, how he’s using A.I. agents and his advice for college graduates.
author: Kevin Roose, Casey Newton and Whitney Jones

title: Our Field Trip to Google I/O + A Sit-Down With Sundar Pichai + System Update
description: “This is the only recent gathering of a large number of people where mentions of A.I. did not produce a large chorus of boos.”
author: Kevin Roose, Casey Newton, Rachel Cohn, Whitney Jones, Vjeran Pavic, Katie McMurran, Dan Powell, Elisheba Ittoop, Rowan Niemisto and Alyssa Moxley

title: ‘Peanuts’ Music Owner Sues Interior Department and 3 Companies for Copyright Infringement
description: The owner of the “Peanuts” catalog would really like it if companies and the U.S. government stopped using its music without permission.
author: Aimee Ortiz

title: Meta Settles a School District’s Social Media Addiction Lawsuit
description: Snap, TikTok and YouTube had already settled with the Kentucky district, allowing the companies to avert the first in a series of federal trials.
author: Cecilia Kang

title: Samsung Averts a Walkout With Big Bonuses, but Discord Over A.I. Profits Brews
description: Samsung Electronics, a global memory chip supplier, has been a major beneficiary of the A.I boom. It has also become the center of a debate over how to divide its spoils.
author: Max Kim

title: Trump Cancels Signing of Executive Order Granting Oversight of A.I. Models
description: The president said he postponed the executive order, which would give the government power to evaluate A.I. models before their release, over concerns about “aspects of it.”
author: Tripp Mickle and Sheera Frenkel
